daily.baseball_pulse:
**Roster moves and injury updates:** MLB's No. 1 prospect Konnor Griffin was reassigned to minor league camp by Pittsburgh—he won't crack the Opening Day roster despite impressive spring performance. More significantly, **Merrill Kelly (Diamondbacks) is headed to the 15-day IL** with a back issue, opening up rotation opportunities. Reese McGuire (Brewers catcher) opted out of his contract and is now a free agent—grab today if available in your league. The Orioles optioned Dean Kremer to minors; monitor for any rotation depth needs. Pirates optioned several relievers. Jasson Domínguez remains optioned by the Yankees despite buzz; add today if you're stashing him for tomorrow activation eligibility.

**Call-ups and promotions to track:** **Ty France's contract was selected by the Padres**—he's now rostered and active for Opening Day at DH/1B. Didier Fuentes made the Braves' Opening Day roster after a strong spring; add today to be active tomorrow if seeking bullpen depth. Brandon Sproat (Brewers) and Parker Messick (Guardians) both made their teams' opening rotations—Messick is the more notable addition as a Top 100 prospect getting a rotation spot. Watch for Jeff Rojas or young Guardians arms if Messick gets early work.

**Pitcher trends:** Chris Sale hit 95 mph in spring action—encouraging velocity news as he enters 2026. Yoshinobu Yamamoto struck out 7 through 3 innings in his spring tune-up, looking sharp for Dodgers. Paul Skenes (Pirates) returned post-WBC and was dominant (4 IP, 1 H, 5 K). Walker Buehler was selected by the Padres and will be in their rotation, offering upside value. Monitor Michael Soroka, Brandon Pfaadt, and Eduardo Rodriguez (Diamondbacks trio) as rotation health developments unfold—Kelly's IL stint could shift opportunity.

**Waiver/streaming intel:** This feed is mostly Spring Breakout prospect showcases (entertaining but not immediately fantasy-relevant for your 12-team league). The actionable intel is limited to the moves noted above. Focus your pre-Opening Day add efforts on McGuire (FA catcher market), Fuentes (bullpen depth), and any borderline rotation prospects getting early-season work. Draft prep: Use custom rankings for your league settings—don't rely on standard rankings heading into draft season.
